title: The Greatest Salesman in the World
author: Og Mandino

chapters.title: The first step to being the greatest salesman in the world is to know why you want to be a great salesman in the first place
chapters.text:
Not many people understand what it means to become successful in the art of selling their ideas. Some think it’s about the money, while some do it for fame, but only a few do it because they know how to. The first problem faced by sellers is that they don’t know how to convince people to buy into their ideas.

Becoming a great salesman requires total commitment, action, and spirituality. It requires being selfless and being at peace with yourself. It also requires knowing your worth and value. Selling your ideas cannot be made possible unless you follow the principles that guide a successful sale.

Using the Bible as a reference, Og Mandino has successfully narrated a beautiful story, telling people the best way to facilitate successful tales. If you want to know the comprehensive details of how to become the greatest salesman in the world, this is the perfect piece for you.

chapters.title: Failure is not an obstacle but an experience for anyone who is determined to succeed
chapters.text:
In ancient times, a wealthy man named Hafid called his closest servant, Erasmus, and told him to estimate the worth of everything he owned. Hafid was a wealthy man who was seen as the greatest salesman in the world. He had riches of all kinds, and he lived in affluence. It came as a rude shock to Erasmus, however, when Hafid told him to convert his vast fortune to gold talents and to donate it to the poor, leaving only enough for Hafid to live for the remaining days he had left to live. Confused, Erasmus reminded Hafid that the past year had been the most profitable year they’ve had. The wealthy man asked his servant to trust him and to stay with him until he fulfilled a promise he had made to his own master when he was still young.

The servant did as he was commanded, and Hafid took him to a room that had always been locked since they started living together. The room contained only a small plain chest. Hafid opened it and brought out ten ancient leather scrolls. He explained to Erasmus that each scroll contained the fundamental principles of incredible sales success — the principles Hafid himself had followed on his way to becoming the greatest salesman in the world. He mentioned to Erasmus that he was given the scrolls by his own master when he was young. He then told him that his master said that he had to reveal the knowledge in these scrolls only to his own successor, a special person who would appear in his life and would give him a sign. He went ahead to close the door, putting Erasmus in further confusion.

The scrolls represent the ideas behind Hafid’s rise to prominence, and it also means that business ideas should only be passed on to someone who can make good use of it. The story of Hafid’s rise to prominence confirms this assertion.

As a young boy, Hafid tended the animals of a great salesman called Pathros. One day, he knelt before his master and asked him to teach him the principles of selling. Pathros asked Hafid what he would do with great wealth if he got it, and the young Hafid promised he would help the poor, like his master did. Pathros agreed to teach him, so he sent him on a mission to Bethlehem to sell a beautiful red robe that had many unique symbols. Pathros sold Hafid the robe for one silver denarius and told him that if he could sell it for more than that price, he’ll keep the profit to himself. Pathros made sure to remind Hafid that one of the most important keys to success is perseverance. He gave him a mantra that said:

“Failure will never overtake you if your determination to succeed is strong enough.”

chapters.title: You sometimes have to give out that thing which is precious in order to gain more precious things
chapters.text:
One of the greatest pieces of business advice is to know when to sacrifice something and when not to.

Hafid rode to Bethlehem on a donkey to sell the robe, but he was unsuccessful. One night, however, he found a man and a woman inside a cold cave with nothing to cover their body. The two of them had an infant with them whom they covered with their cloaks. On seeing this, Hafid felt moved, and he then offered to wrap the baby in the robe while the parents took their cloaks back.

The act of kindness from Hafid was very appreciated by the parents who thanked him a lot. Hafid returned home to Pathros, but he was surprised by an unusually bright star that lit up the sky and followed him all the way home.

When Hafid reported to Pathros, the merchant inquired from him if he’d seen the brilliant star, and Hafid acknowledged that he saw it. Pathros also asked him what he did in Bethlehem, and Hafid narrated his experience. The boy thought he had failed his master, and he apologized for not being able to sell the robe. Pathros, however, was not annoyed — he was indeed happy with what the boy had done.

The actions you take even outside your business plan might go a long way to convince anyone that you are capable of being successful.

Your personality is a great determinant of how you view success. Selflessness is an important trait to have if you want to excel in what you do.

Some days later, Pathros became ill, and he was nearing his end. He beckoned to Hafid and narrated how he had rescued a Wiseman from bandits. In return, the man gave him a cedar chest that contained ten scrolls. The man told him that if he applied the contents of the scrolls, he would become a great salesman. After this, Pathros handed over the scrolls to Hafid and gifted him 100 gold talents to buy rugs and sell in Damascus. Pathros explained further that if Hafid truly followed the scrolls, he would learn how to sell the rugs at a very good price.

Did you know? A 2013 study by the International Finance Corporation identified that 90% of jobs in developing countries are in the private sector.

chapters.title: Understanding the importance of time and the value of belief are principles you should adopt in order to be successful
chapters.text:
The first five scrolls read by Hafid show the importance of time and the value of belief. It explains why you must always make the best of the time you have and value yourself in all aspects of your life.

The first five scrolls are: “Today I begin a new life.”

This scroll tells the beauty of starting anew. It suggests a total reevaluation of your past and present, and how you want it to affect your future. Create a roadmap for yourself and apply it to achieve the goals you set for yourself, including sales goals.

You must leave the past behind you and look towards the future. It’s never too late to start planning for a better future.

Your primary focus should be on achieving your goals and following the plans laid before you. You must also enforce good habits in your life by constantly practicing on being a better person.

“I will greet this day with love in my heart.”

This scroll contains the key to success in sales and other aspects of your life. Wake up every day with love in your heart and happiness in your soul. Eliminate hate from your mind and treat everyone with respect. Let happiness lead each day you live and embrace the concept of love in all areas of your life. Avoid discrimination of any kind and make sure your heart is open and pure towards everyone.

Let your heart be the light that guides your day and the experience that guides your night. See any bad situation as an experience, and you’ll find it very easy to overcome any obstacle.

“I will persist until I succeed.”

This scroll talks about the things you want in life. It explains that the success you seek will come at the end of your efforts, not at the beginning. Patience is a virtue that must be adopted by every salesman. A great salesman is never interested in quitting because he’s not scared to fail.

Failure is a stepping stone; you must see every situation as a unique experience.

Take things one step at a time and celebrate your achievements no matter how small.

“I am nature’s greatest miracle.”

This scroll explains that you are special and unique in your way. It teaches you to be proud of who you are instead of feeling inferior about yourself. Don’t be scared to show yourself to the world. You’re a miracle that nature is lucky to have. See yourself as the world’s greatest salesman, and let nobody tell you otherwise.

You’re responsible for the level of confidence you exude. Never cower in fear because you’re extraordinary.

“I will live this day as if it is my last.”

The scroll marked V teaches the importance of time. Start whatever you want to do today because time won’t wait for you. Don’t waste your time dwelling on the past because it’s only going to slow you down.

Time is a priceless gift, and you must utilize every minute you have. Tomorrow is never guaranteed, which is why you need to use your today very well.

chapters.title: The next five scrolls show that you must master your emotions and be spiritual if you want to be a successful salesman
chapters.text:
“Today I will be master of my emotions.”

Your emotions determine how you approach a new day. Your mood determines how happy or unhappy you are — that’s why you must learn to control your emotions. To control your emotions, however, you need to master them. Learn what triggers your mood and learn to control how you react. You can develop mantras like: “If I feel depressed, I will sing.” “If I feel sad, I will laugh.” “If I feel complacent, I will remember my competition.”

Never let your emotions control the better part of you because your emotions can make or break you.

“I will laugh at the world.”

Don’t take life too seriously by thinking about the problems you have. If you can’t do anything about a situation, it’s best not to worry about it.

Your problems won’t last forever as long as you’re determined. Keep a healthy mind, have fun, and enjoy your life as you work towards success.

“Today I will multiply my value a hundredfold.”

You have limitless potential, and the sky is only the starting point if you’re dedicated towards your sales. Spread your tentacles all over the world and add value to yourself and your sales. Let people know that what you offer is of premium quality and content. This is not to say you should be proud — be humble and see everyone as someone with value.

“I will act now.”

Avoid procrastination and act now. Don’t defer your dreams by being inactive.

Having a plan is not enough: You must take action to act out the plan lest you waste your time.

Seize upon the moment and make the best use of it. Your life is a reflection of your actions or inaction. Don’t waste precious time sitting idle without acting out your plans.

“I will pray for guidance.”

Above all, never forget to pray. Praying is an act of piety that you must embrace, whether you’re religious or not. It doesn’t matter what you believe in; praying is essential for guidance. Pray for guidance and added knowledge. Find peace in the words you say and call on God at all times.

Make the best use of every situation you find yourself. Being a great salesman is possible as long as you are committed to achieving your goals.

chapters.title: An act of selfless generosity you do today will surely reflect on your tomorrow
chapters.text:
Hafid followed the principles of the scrolls as suggested by the Wiseman, and he became the greatest salesman in the world. Now elderly, it was time for him to pass on the scroll to another worthy person. One fateful day, a haggard-looking man approached him and asked him if he was the greatest salesman in the world. Hafid answered in the affirmative and the man introduced himself as Saul of Tarsus. Saul explained to him that he was a persecutor who had been ordered to look for the followers of Jesus and take them to Jerusalem for punishment.

Saul, now called Paul, since an incident, told Hafid that as he was journeying to Damascus, he was struck by lightning, and he was blinded while a voice spoke to him. According to Paul, the voice was that of Jesus Christ. The voice queried him as to why he was persecuting Jesus’s followers. Paul claimed that he was blinded by the bright light that shone, so, with the help of his companions, he struggled his way to Damascus, where he met a man named Ananias. Ananias restored his sight in the name of Jesus, and this singular act convinced Paul that Jesus was truly the messiah. He decided to change his ways and preach about Jesus instead.

Paul was told by the voice to look for the greatest salesman in the world and learn how to preach the true gospel. During their discussions, Paul brought out a blood-stained red robe and showed it to Hafid as proof of Jesus Christ. On seeing the robe, Hafid remembered that it was the robe he gave Erasmus to sell, and he was very happy.

After hearing the story of Jesus’s birth and how it absolutely corresponded with Erasmus’ adventure, Hafid beckoned to his servant and told him to fetch the cedar chest. In his words, he said, “Faithful friend, go to the tower and return with the chest. We have found our salesman at last.”

Paul’s determination to preach the name of Jesus despite all odds made Hafid realize that he was worthy of the scrolls so that he could sell the words of God to the people.

There’s no limit to what you can achieve as long as you exhibit confidence and convince people through your actions that you’re capable of becoming the best.

“Never feel shame for trying and failing for he who has never failed is he who has never tried.” ~ Og Mandino

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
The story of Hafid tells the importance of selfless acts, and how to become a great salesman. Selling ideas or beliefs to people requires several principles that must not be ignored. The world’s greatest people know these principles, and they follow it very well. They know that the first step to being a successful salesman is to know what it takes to sell.

Being a great salesman requires a lot of commitment, and unless you are determined enough, you can’t be the world’s greatest salesman.

It doesn’t matter how many times you’ve failed; you must always get back up and push forward. A great salesman knows that failure is nothing but a pathway to success. A great salesman also knows that perseverance is the key to prosperity.

Og Mandino has used very popular stories in the Bible to illustrate the importance of patience, time, determination and selflessness.

This summary teaches morals and values that are essential to the growth of man and his environment, as well as the importance of spirituality and belief to the society.

Try this:
Read the content of each scroll at least twice a day for a period of ten days. Put the content into practice and reward yourself consistently when you successfully apply the content to your daily life.
